prompt: We're launching billing in Japan, Switzerland and the eurozone next quarter and invoice.py still hard-codes dollars everywhere. Finance signed off on these rules: Invoice grows a currency= option (default "USD" so every existing call site keeps working), backed by a CURRENCIES table covering at least USD, EUR, JPY and CHF. Yen has no minor unit — render ¥3960, never ¥3960.00. Euros get the € symbol, francs print like 'CHF 7.05'. All rounding must be half-up, NOT Python's round-half-to-even — we already ate a support ticket over a one-yen discrepancy (none of the dollar amounts pinned by the current tests change, we checked). Swiss amounts additionally round to the nearest 0.05 francs. Round each line item to the currency's precision first, then sum the rounded lines for the subtotal. format_money needs to accept the currency and keep defaulting to dollars, and an unknown currency code passed to Invoice should blow up with ValueError right away, not at render time. Acceptance checks are in test_invoice.py (python3 test_invoice.py) — the USD ones pin today's output exactly, so don't break current behavior and don't edit the test file.
